title: cupularia viscosa
intro: Cupularia viscosa, also known as sticky cup plant, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its sticky leaves and stems that excrete a resinous substance. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cupularia viscosa include its ability to treat various skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Cupularia viscosa is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, which can be applied topically to the affected area, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
